About
Changing lines

Changing Lines Guide

Changing lines show where movement appears inside an I Ching reading and how the primary hexagram begins to shift.

What changing lines mean

Changing lines are the parts of the reading that are not stable. They point to pressure, movement, or adjustment inside the current situation.

How to read them

Read the primary hexagram first, then use the changing lines to understand where the situation is active. Do not treat each line as a separate unrelated answer.

How they connect to the relating hexagram

When changing lines transform, they create the relating hexagram. This gives a second layer of direction, but it should stay connected to the original pattern.

Try the tool

Cast a reading and compare the result with this guide.

Open tool
